metadata.title: Build a Brand Palette Accessibility Auditor with Claude
metadata.note: cli-ideas Card 21 — Ch 9 (Visual Identity Systems). DOUBLE-CHECK: WCAG 2.2 AA thresholds: normal text 4.5:1, large text/graphical objects 3:1. Formula: L = 0.2126R + 0.7152G + 0.0722B on linearized channels; ratio = (L_lighter + 0.05)/(L_darker + 0.05). warm-clay #A89068 on white #FFFFFF: ratio ≈ 3.1:1 — FAIL for normal text, PASS for AA-large. Ch 9 example: caption text #9CA3AF on #FAFAF8 fails at 2.9:1, fix: darken to #6B7280 → 4.6:1. Corrected alternative for warm-clay: near #7A6045 achieving ≥4.5:1.

source_of_truth: beat_sheet.json — edit the sheet, not this file

beats.label: This is Liam, in for Bear. Build it with Claude, then take it apart.

beats.prompt: A palette that fails WCAG AA cannot be read by some fraction of your audience. A color combination below four-point-five to one contrast ratio for normal text i
beats.label: A palette that fails WCAG AA cannot be read by some fraction of your audience. A color combination b
Into Claude. Given palette hex codes and intended text-on-background pairings, compute the WCAG cont
Read it. The luminance calculation is the critical path. The linearization step is non-obvious — cha
Run it on a warm archetype palette including warm-clay A-8-9-0-6-8 on white. Table prints: six pairi
Apply the corrected hex values and re-run the audit to verify. Then check the non-text elements — ic
Re-run. The corrected palette prints five of six text pairings passing AA for normal text. The sixth
